forecast-period.text: A cold front over eastern districts will move to the east of SA overnight. The low pressure system associated with this front is expected to move slowly eastwards over waters to the south of the state and will extend a trough over southern parts of SA later Sunday and Monday. The low will then move over Bass Strait later Monday. A ridge will extend across SA from the west on Tuesday and a cold front will move across the south later Wednesday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Spencer Gulf
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Spencer Gulf
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Cowell to Wallaroo during the day. Winds turning westerly in the early afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers south of Cowell to Wallaroo, 50% chance elsewhere.
Westerly 20 to 30 knots decreasing to 15 to 25 knots before dawn then tending southwesterly 15 to 20 knots in the middle of the day.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
South to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots becoming variable about 10 knots during the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Cloudy.
